creator: mav@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 15.01.2013 12:40, Heikki Suonsivu wrote:
> On 1/15/13 11:15 AM, Alexander Motin wrote:
>> I am unable to reproduce the problem on fresh system with 3TB drives.
>> Either the problem is not in gmirror itself (SATA controller, driver,
>> some RAID BIOS, etc) or it is already fixed somehow.
> 
> I think I managed to get rid of this by dd zeroes after boot block until
> freebsd partition, apparently there was something after the MBR  and
> before bsd label which confused FreeBSD.  It is likely that those disks
> had been used in dedicated mode before, and then partitioned without
> clearing beginning of the disk, so there probably was some UFS data or
> like after MBR.  It might be a good idea to figure this out as there
> should be no reason for freebsd to read anything from area which is not
> used at all.  Unfortunately I did not take a copy of the offending data,
> I just dd:t it over.

There are some known problems from metadata detection order in GEOM.
Some classes, such as ufs or ufsid LABEL, due to lack of strict
information in their metadata may grab disk areas not belonging to them,
preventing MIRROR to attach. That may be not a MIRROR problem.

-- 
Alexander Motin
